title: Sample preparation and imaging for large scale 3D spectral confocal imaging of tissues
author: Tarek M. El-Achkar
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.9avh2e6

steps.title: Mount sample on uncharged slide in...
steps.instruction: Mount sample on uncharged slide in PBS using prepositioned piece of spacer tape to support coverslip.

steps.title: Gently push coverslip to the sample...
steps.instruction: Gently push coverslip to the sample and seal with rubber cement dispensed from a syringe and 16 G needle.

steps.title: Configure the microscope for multiphoton excitation...
steps.instruction: Configure the microscope for multiphoton excitation at 910 nm for detecting SHG and auto-fluorescence (AF) (Table 2). SP8 confocal scan-head mounted upright DM6000 microscope (Confocal Microscope, Leica, n/a, Custom motorized stage coupled to a femtosecond pulsing MaiTai DeepSee laser (Spectra Physics, Santa Clara, CA) with EOM modulation for multiphoton excitation and a solid state laser launch providing 405 nm, 488 nm, 552 nm and 635 nm excitation. Imaging is performed with 25x 0.95 NA or 20x 0.75 NA Mimm objective with water immersion for label-free imaging or Leica immersion oil RI=1.51 with a 20x 0.75 NA Mimm objective for spectral confocal imaging. Detectors on the SP8 include two HyD detectors and two photo-multiplier tube (PMT) detectors ordered by wavelength, HyD1, PMT2, HyD3, PMT4. The SP8 is controlled by and linear unmixing is performed in Leica LASX software v. 3.5.2 build 18963.)

steps.title: Configure emission path by opening confocal...
steps.instruction: Configure emission path by opening confocal pinhole and adjust the bandpasses on 2 to 4 detectors to collect SHG (~455 nm) and AF as desired (refer to “Excitation and filter settings”). Use PMTs to avoid detector “overloading” and data loss.

steps.title: Configure x, y and z limits...
steps.instruction: Configure x, y and z limits to cover the entire tissue. Scan in x and y at 0.5-1 µm/pixel. Sample in z with 1-2 um steps.

steps.title: Perform scans with standard galvanometers set...
steps.instruction: Perform scans with standard galvanometers set to 400 Hz, and averaging by line 2x.

steps.title: Fluorescence lifetime imaging is under development...
steps.instruction: Fluorescence lifetime imaging is under development and may be included at this step.

steps.title: Recover specimen by removing rubber cement and gently lifting coverslip
steps.instruction: Recover specimen by removing rubber cement and gently lifting coverslip. Lifting the coverslip may be easier if done after or while soaking the slide in PBS.

steps.title: Washing
steps.instruction: Washing: Sample is washed 1x in PBS > 00:10:00 Phosphate buffered saline pH 7.4 (PBS) Contributed by users

steps.title: Blocking
steps.instruction: Blocking: Remove wash and block non-specific binding sites with PBSTS 4–8 hours at Room temperature on rocking platform. PBSTS (PBST & 10% NDS) Jackson ImmunoResearch Laboratories, Inc. Catalog #017-000-121

steps.title: Indirect primary antibody staining (Table 1)
steps.instruction: Indirect primary antibody staining (Table 1): Incubate tissue in 200 µL staining solution for all the indirect detection primary antibodies 8-16 hours at Room temperature on rocking platform.

steps.title: Washing
steps.instruction: Washing: Wash sample with 2 mL PBST 2 times for a total of 06:00:00 . PBST (PBS & 0.1% Triton X-100) MP Biomedicals Catalog #807423

steps.title: Blocking
steps.instruction: Blocking: Remove wash and block non-specific binding sites with PBSTS 04:00:00 to 08:00:00 at Room temperature on rocking platform. PBSTS (PBST & 10% NDS) Jackson ImmunoResearch Laboratories, Inc. Catalog #017-000-121

steps.title: Indirect secondary antibody staining (Table 1)
steps.instruction: Indirect secondary antibody staining (Table 1): Incubate tissue in 200 µL staining solution for all the indirect detection secondary antibodies 08:00:00 to 16:00:00 at Room temperature on rocking platform.

steps.title: Washing
steps.instruction: Washing: Wash sample with 2 mL PBST twice for a total of 06:00:00 . PBST (PBS & 0.1% Triton X-100) MP Biomedicals Catalog #807423

steps.title: Blocking
steps.instruction: Blocking: Remove wash and block non-specific binding sites with PBSTS 04:00:00 to 08:00:00 at Room temperature on rocking platform.

steps.title: Directly conjugated antibody staining (Table 1)
steps.instruction: Directly conjugated antibody staining (Table 1): Incubate tissue in 200 µL staining solution for all the direct detection primary antibodies 08:00:00 to 16:00:00 at Room temperature on rocking platform.

steps.title: Washing
steps.instruction: Washing: Wash sample with 2 mL PBST twice for a total of 06:00:00 . PBST (PBS & 0.1% Triton X-100) MP Biomedicals Catalog #807423

steps.title: Mount tissue with Prolong Glass or...
steps.instruction: Mount tissue with Prolong Glass or Fluoromount on pre-cleaned sides with silicone grease as needed to support coverslip. Fluoromount Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #F4680 Glass slides Contributed by users Catalog #3017 Coverglass #1.5 22X22mm Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #12-541-B

steps.title: Seal cover slip to slide with...
steps.instruction: Seal cover slip to slide with rubber cement applied with a syringe.

steps.title: Configure the microscope for spectral confocal...
steps.instruction: Configure the microscope for spectral confocal imaging as described in Table 2 below. The settings for laser power, detector, voltage, gain and offset may vary based on the microscope configuration.

steps.title: Configure x, y and z limits...
steps.instruction: Configure x, y and z limits to cover the entire tissue. Scan in x and y at 0.5-1 µm/pixel. Sample in z with 1 µm steps.

steps.title: Perform scans with standard galvanometers set to 400 Hz
steps.instruction: Perform scans with standard galvanometers set to 400 Hz. The multispectral 16 channels are configured as indicated in Table 2 with 4 sequences of 4 detectors.

steps.title: Incubate 20 µL of CML latex...
steps.instruction: Incubate 20 µL of CML latex beads (ThermoFisher, cat# C37253) with 100 µg x g, °C, of fluorophore conjugated secondary overnight. Reference spectra for DAPI and Oregon Green are collected from tissue labeled only with DAPI or Oregon Green. The remaining reference spectra from fluorophores outlined in Table 1 are collected from single-fluorophore labeled latex beads.

steps.title: Wash the beads after centrifuging at...
steps.instruction: Wash the beads after centrifuging at 1000 x g, Room temperature, 30 seconds . Repeat twice. Dilute and mix individually labeled beads in MM (Prolong Glass or Fluoromount) such that 5-10 beads of each individually labeled beads is visible in a field size of 500 x 500 µm.

steps.title: Beads are mounted under #1
steps.instruction: Beads are mounted under #1.5 coverglass and cured for 48:00:00 to 96:00:00 Coverglass #1.5 22X22mm Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #12-541-B

steps.title: Scan slide-mounted beads labeled with known...
steps.instruction: Scan slide-mounted beads labeled with known fluorophores with settings given in the table below for channels 1-16. Data collected is subjected to linear unmixing with Leica LASX software

steps.title: Select “Process” -> “Dye Separation” -> “Channel Dye Separation”
steps.instruction: Select “Process” -> “Dye Separation” -> “Channel Dye Separation”

steps.title: Select regions of interest including beads...
steps.instruction: Select regions of interest including beads intensity and select “Add” to add to spectra.

steps.title: Select Rescale to “Per Channel
steps.instruction: Select Rescale to “Per Channel.”

steps.title: Select “Load” to load a saved matrix
steps.instruction: Select “Load” to load a saved matrix. The saved matrix will be displayed near the bottom of the window.

steps.title: Select “Apply”
steps.instruction: Select “Apply”.

steps.title: In the LASX software select image to unmix
steps.instruction: In the LASX software select image to unmix. Once a matrix file is generated (.sdm) it may be used again to apply collected images.

steps.title: Select “Process” -> “Dye Separation” -> “Automatic Dye Separation”
steps.instruction: Select “Process” -> “Dye Separation” -> “Automatic Dye Separation”

steps.title: Set fluorescent dyes to “8
steps.instruction: Set fluorescent dyes to “8.”

steps.title: Set Rescale to “Per Channel
steps.instruction: Set Rescale to “Per Channel.”

steps.title: Select “Load” to load a saved matrix
steps.instruction: Select “Load” to load a saved matrix. The saved matrix will be displayed near the bottom of the window.

steps.title: Select “Apply”
steps.instruction: Select “Apply”.

steps.title: Select images to merge
steps.instruction: Select images to merge. Following unmixing, the confocal dataset is merged with LEICA LASX software.

steps.title: Select “Process” -> “Mosaic Merge”
steps.instruction: Select “Process” -> “Mosaic Merge”

steps.title: Check “Autostitching”, “Smooth” overlap and “Linear Blending”
steps.instruction: Check “Autostitching”, “Smooth” overlap and “Linear Blending”.

steps.title: Select “Apply”
steps.instruction: Select “Apply”.

steps.title: All 3D cytometry is performed with...
steps.instruction: All 3D cytometry is performed with the ImageJ plugin Volumetric Tissue Exploration and Analysis (VTEA). The settings for processing, segmentation and gating are given in analysis files. As software improves, new versions of VTEA may be used.
